relationships.label: Treat the station as staged
relationships.notes: The player reads the drag marks as the first real break: Rowan may have been killed or disabled somewhere else.

gameStory.choiceText: Treat the station as a staged body dump
gameStory.consequenceNotes: Opens the clean investigation route and makes Silas more useful later.

relationships.label: Follow the station history
relationships.notes: The player starts with the obvious old-station suspect before fully proving the body was moved.

gameStory.consequenceNotes: This keeps the case moving, but makes the frame route tempting.

relationships.label: Ask who knows the old station
relationships.notes: The staging theory points toward someone who understands the station's symbolic geography.

gameStory.consequenceNotes: Frames Silas as a knowledge gate rather than just a suspect.

relationships.label: Follow the drag route
relationships.notes: The player follows the drag marks back toward the service road and then into the signal room.

gameStory.choiceText: Follow the drag marks toward the signal room
gameStory.consequenceNotes: Reaches the right room without the key, which still leaves the ledger route gated.

relationships.label: Earn the Locker 12 key
relationships.notes: Silas helps when the player can name the staging evidence before accusing him.

gameStory.consequenceNotes: Silas reveals that Rowan asked him about Locker 12 two days before the murder.

relationships.label: Pressure the obvious suspect
relationships.notes: The player can still advance, but Silas withholds the key and the case tilts toward a false frame.

gameStory.choiceText: Accuse Silas and search without his help
gameStory.consequenceNotes: Keeps momentum but blocks the easiest path to the ledger.

relationships.label: Open Locker 12
relationships.notes: The key route reveals Rowan's actual secret and points the confrontation at Evelyn.

gameStory.choiceText: Use the key and read the signal ledger
gameStory.consequenceNotes: The ledger proves the body was placed near Platform 7 to frame a station insider and recover missing evidence.

relationships.label: Confront without the ledger
relationships.notes: The player challenges Evelyn with incomplete proof and risks accepting the frame.

gameStory.choiceText: Confront Evelyn with the station clues alone
gameStory.consequenceNotes: Useful for a pressure scene, but it leaves the true motive deniable.

relationships.label: Prove Evelyn staged the body
relationships.notes: The player connects drag marks, locker evidence, and Rowan's argument into the true accusation.

gameStory.choiceText: Present the ledger and the drag-route proof
gameStory.consequenceNotes: Truth route: Evelyn killed Rowan after he found the ledger and moved the body to retrieve it and frame Silas.

relationships.label: Accept the Silas frame
relationships.notes: The player closes the case around the station myth instead of the redevelopment motive.

gameStory.consequenceNotes: Bad but playable ending: the station keeps its false legend and the true murder location stays hidden.

relationships.label: Needs Locker 12 key
relationships.notes: The clean evidence route requires getting Silas to reveal where Rowan left the key.

gameStory.consequenceNotes: World-graph reminder that the branch is gated by trust.

relationships.label: Unlocks the redevelopment confrontation
relationships.notes: Whether or not the ledger is found, the signal room gives enough structure for a confrontation scene.

gameStory.consequenceNotes: Structural link for the Story Flow graph; branch links carry the actual choices.

relationships.label: Investigates Rowan's death
relationships.notes: Arden enters as a procedural outsider whose only leverage is noticing what the scene refuses to explain.

relationships.label: Body found at the station
relationships.notes: Publicly true but misleading: Rowan's body is found here, not necessarily killed here.

relationships.label: Found in fresh dust
relationships.notes: The prints are fresh enough to be from the night Rowan was moved.

relationships.label: Cross the platform threshold
relationships.notes: The marks run into the station, which pushes against the first easy read of the crime scene.

relationships.label: Contradicts station-as-murder-site
relationships.notes: The body position and drag marks suggest the station is a stage, not the primary attack location.

relationships.label: Last public sighting
relationships.notes: A witness heard Rowan arguing near the service road before the body was found.

relationships.label: Heard arguing with Rowan
relationships.notes: The voice is not confirmed at first, but later lines up with Evelyn's meeting schedule.

relationships.label: Chairs the demolition vote
relationships.notes: Evelyn has authority over site access and the motive to keep old records buried.

relationships.label: Wants the station demolished
relationships.notes: The board's schedule puts pressure on the investigation before the scene can be fully processed.

relationships.label: Threatened by Rowan's evidence
relationships.notes: Rowan's quiet maintenance work becomes dangerous once he finds the Platform 7 ledger.

relationships.label: Hid the locker key
relationships.notes: Rowan had the key, but it does not stay on his body after the killer searches him.

relationships.label: Opens Locker 12
relationships.notes: Silas can point the player toward the key if he believes Arden is not there to close a frame.

relationships.label: Inside the signal room
relationships.notes: The locker is close enough to the body placement to matter, but not obvious from the platform.

relationships.label: Hidden in Locker 12
relationships.notes: The ledger is the reason Rowan knew the abandoned station still mattered.

relationships.label: Links Evelyn to the cover-up
relationships.notes: The ledger ties the old Platform 7 false reports to the current demolition schedule.

relationships.label: Beside Rowan's body
relationships.notes: The marker makes the placement look symbolic enough to frame a station obsessive.

relationships.label: Points to Platform 7 records
relationships.notes: The marker is the bridge between the body placement and the hidden ledger.

relationships.label: Former signalman
relationships.notes: Silas knows the station well enough to look guilty, which is exactly why he is useful as a frame.

relationships.label: Matches Jessa's boots
relationships.notes: A red herring: Jessa trespassed after the body was moved and saw more than she admits.

relationships.label: Urban explorer trespass
relationships.notes: Jessa's presence explains some footprints while preserving the killer's separate drag route.

relationships.label: Drag route begins outside
relationships.notes: The drag route suggests Rowan was carried or dragged from a vehicle near the service road.

relationships.label: Back way into the station
relationships.notes: The culvert gives the killer a quiet route that avoids the front gates.

relationships.label: Triggers playable opening
relationships.notes: The world timeline event is the canon source for the first playable crime-scene scene.

relationships.label: Triggers staged-body quest
relationships.notes: The physical contradiction turns into the main investigation quest once the player treats the station as staged.

relationships.label: Triggers witness interview
relationships.notes: The last public argument gives the Silas interview a concrete world-building reason to exist.

relationships.label: Triggers signal-room search
relationships.notes: The world location becomes playable when the investigation route points the player toward Locker 12.

relationships.label: Triggers Evelyn confrontation
relationships.notes: The ledger is the world evidence that turns suspicion into a playable accusation scene.
